queue.title: Implement IDA* with ZeroHeuristic
queue.prompt: Implement IDA* using the existing SearchSolution and Heuristic abstractions. Start with ZeroHeuristic support, keep the implementation bounded by max_depth, and use only FACE_MOVES for search expansion.
acceptance: solved cube returns an empty solution
1-move and 2-move scrambles solve within depth limit
insufficient max_depth returns None
explored_nodes is reported
the returned solution actually solves the cube
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
queue.commitMessage: Implement IDA* search with zero heuristic

queue.title: Wire IDA* to simple heuristics
queue.prompt: Use the existing simple heuristics with IDA*. Add tests showing MaxHeuristic can guide IDA* on shallow scrambles without changing correctness. Keep ZeroHeuristic behavior intact.
acceptance: IDA* accepts any Heuristic implementation
MaxHeuristic solves shallow scrambles
ZeroHeuristic tests still pass
all solutions remain valid and use FACE_MOVES
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Parse and validate user facelet states
queue.prompt: Add a user-facing facelet/sticker parser for 3x3 cube states. Support a stable 54-character Kociemba-style facelet string first. Validate length, allowed face symbols, and exact color counts before conversion to cubies.
acceptance: solved facelet string parses successfully
invalid length is rejected with a useful error
invalid symbols are rejected with a useful error
invalid face/color counts are rejected
the parser does not replace CubieState as the engine representation
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Convert facelets to validated CubieState
queue.prompt: Convert parsed 54-facelet cube states into CubieState. Reuse CubieState validation for piece uniqueness, orientation, permutation, and parity. Return clear errors for impossible cube states.
acceptance: solved facelets convert to CubieState::solved
valid generated states round-trip through facelets where supported
impossible swapped pieces are rejected
invalid orientation/parity states are rejected
conversion output can be passed to Cube::try_from_state
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Add public solve API for user states
queue.prompt: Add a public Rust solve API that accepts CubieState or facelet input, validates it, runs the best available solver with configurable max_depth/max_nodes, and returns a verified solution plus metrics. It may use IDA* initially while deeper Kociemba work follows.
acceptance: solved state returns an empty solution
known shallow facelet state returns a solution
invalid user state returns validation errors instead of panicking
returned moves are replayed internally to verify the submitted state is solved
solution metrics include length and explored_nodes
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Add Kociemba coordinate indexing
queue.prompt: Add Kociemba/two-phase coordinate indexing utilities needed for phase 1 and phase 2 search. Start with orientation and slice-related coordinates, keep them pure Rust, deterministic, and heavily tested.
acceptance: coordinates encode solved state to zero or documented solved coordinate
coordinates round-trip where decoding is implemented
coordinates distinguish relevant phase constraints
invalid coordinates are rejected
no large generated artifacts are committed

queue.title: Implement Kociemba phase 1 search
queue.prompt: Implement phase 1 of a Kociemba-style two-phase solver. Search should transform valid cube states into the phase 2 subgroup using admissible pruning where available, while reporting metrics and respecting configured limits.
acceptance: solved state has phase 1 length zero
known shallow scrambles reach phase 2 subgroup
phase 1 respects max_depth/max_nodes limits
phase 1 output is replayed and verified
phase 1 uses allowed move sets documented in code

queue.prompt: Implement phase 2 search for states already in the Kociemba phase 2 subgroup. Combine phase 1 and phase 2 candidates into full verified solutions.

combined phase 1 + phase 2 solution solves known scrambles
combined solver reports total length and metrics
combined solver respects configured limits

queue.title: Add two-phase pruning tables

solved coordinates have distance zero
lookups are admissible for shallow known distances
